page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with hyperthreading enabled, utilizing an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, while multi-core performance falls into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is relatively constrained, with read-only small block cached bandwidth scoring in the bottom 10% and uncached large block read/write bandwidth in the bottom 25%. Text processing and single-threaded compression tasks perform at average levels, whereas database operations, software compilation, and redis SET operations show poor performance, ranking in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c2d-highmem-2 offers a high memory allocation relative to its low vCPU count, making it a resource-dense option for workloads requiring memory capacity over raw parallel computing power. Given the weak multi-core and database benchmark results, it is less suited for heavy database engines or intensive multi-threaded compilation. Instead, it is best aligned with lightweight memory-bound applications, single-threaded text processing, basic web serving, or small-scale LLM text generation tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations rank in the lower tiers. Consequently, this instance provides a cost-efficient profile for memory-bound, single-threaded applications, lightweight text processing, and basic web serving, while being less suited for heavy database workloads or multi-threaded software compilation.

tagline: A memory-optimized dual-vCPU instance featuring AMD EPYC processing, tailored for lightweight, memory-bound applications and single-threaded tasks.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core for memory-bound tasks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the configuration
Best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and single-threaded workloads
